evals.prompt: How do I implement graceful shutdown in a Go HTTP server?
evals.expected_output: Implement a GracefulStop method on the server struct. Listen for os.Signal (SIGTERM, SIGINT) in a goroutine. Call server.Shutdown with a context.WithTimeout (e.g. 10 seconds). Never put signal handling in main() — it belongs in Run() on the server struct. Always set a shutdown timeout to avoid hanging indefinitely.

evals.prompt: What's the difference between /healthz and /readyz endpoints in a Go service?
evals.expected_output: healthz is a liveness probe — returns 200 if the process is alive, no dependency checks. readyz is a readiness probe — checks real dependencies (DB, cache, external services) before returning 200. Never make /readyz always return 200. Kubernetes uses readyz to decide whether to route traffic.

evals.prompt: How should I structure the startup sequence of a Go HTTP server?
evals.expected_output: Initialize logger first, then config, then dependencies (DB, cache), then wire the service graph, finally start the server. Use a Config struct to pass all dependencies to the server. Call log.Init() and defer log.Sync() before anything else. Errors during startup should propagate up to main() which calls log.Fatalw.
